summary: DHH announces his upcoming appearance at the JAOO conference in Århus, Denmark, where he'll deliver a double-session on Ruby on Rails to an audience primarily composed of Java and .NET developers. He frames it as an 'interesting' challenge to present Rails to this enterprise-oriented crowd. The first session will be a live demo, while the second will address common skeptical questions. He sarcastically references the perennial 'But does it scale?' question.
key_points: DHH was actively evangelizing Rails at enterprise-focused conferences dominated by Java and .NET developers
The 'But does it scale?' question was already a recurring challenge Rails faced from the enterprise community in 2005
Live demos were a key part of DHH's Rails advocacy strategy, showing rather than telling
money_quotes: a double-session Ruby on Rails cocktail
It's going to be an interesting audience to present in front.
we'll be answering such marvelous questions as "But does it scale?"
key_insight: Even in 2005, DHH was taking Rails directly into enterprise territory, meeting Java and .NET skepticism head-on with live demos and a confident, slightly combative attitude toward the inevitable scalability questions.
